findings.title: Reviewer lane can mint writeback authority first and clear review by spoofing parent reviewer
findings.rationale: The new guard only prevents reminting once a snapshot file already exists and infers child-lane identity from user-supplied reviewer strings. There is no caller-context check on capture-review-truth-snapshot or record-review-outcome, so a reviewer lane that runs before parent capture can obtain the plaintext writeback token, emit valid reviewer-output artifacts, set reviewer to a non-child label like parent-review-loop, and successfully clear the blocking review gate.
findings.suggested_next_action: Bind capture-review-truth-snapshot and record-review-outcome to authenticated parent caller context (for example trusted lane role/task-path claim propagated into internal commands), then add a regression that simulates a child lane capturing truth first and attempting a spoofed parent writeback with canonical reviewer-output refs; expect rejection.
